title: Grafana Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Data Sources, Panels, and Dashboards as Code.

questions.question: What is the primary relationship between Prometheus and Grafana?

options.text: Grafana replaces Prometheus entirely.
Grafana acts as the database that permanently stores metrics scraped by Prometheus.
Prometheus collects and stores the time-series data, while Grafana connects to Prometheus to query and visualize that data.
Prometheus builds dashboards, and Grafana sends Slack alerts.

questions.question: Which of the following describes Grafana's capability regarding Data Sources?

options.text: Grafana can only connect to Prometheus.
Grafana can connect to multiple disparate data sources (like Prometheus, PostgreSQL, and CloudWatch) on the same dashboard.
Grafana limits you to exactly one Data Source per server installation.
Grafana embeds its own SQLite database to avoid needing external Data Sources.

questions.question: What is the benefit of using Variables (Templating) in a Grafana dashboard?

options.text: It encrypts the queries before sending them to the database.

It allows you to create a single dynamic dashboard with a dropdown menu to switch context (like between different servers or environments) instead of building a static dashboard for every single resource.
It automatically translates PromQL into SQL.
It limits user access based on their IAM role.

questions.question: In a robust DevOps setup, how should Grafana Dashboards be managed to prevent data loss if the Grafana server is deleted?

options.text: By manually clicking 'Export' every Friday and saving the file to your desktop.
By storing the Dashboard JSON definitions in a Git repository and using 'Provisioning' to automatically load them into Grafana.
By running an SQL dump of the Prometheus database.
By taking a screenshot of the UI once a week.

questions.question: Which Grafana Panel type is best suited for plotting the statistical distribution of API response latencies to find outliers?

options.text: Gauge
Stat
Pie Chart
Heatmap
